_about: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the fak CAPABILITY MATRIX (visuals/59-hero-capability-matrix.svg), rendered by tools/hero_matrix_gen.py. fak's analog of the MiniMax-M3 model-comparison table: capabilities grouped by category down the left, the serving stacks across the top, the subject (fak) column washed + highlighted. The thesis is structural and honest: fak carries a value down the WHOLE matrix; the SOTA serving stacks light up only the SERVING REUSE and SINGLE-STREAM rows and carry an honest em-dash everywhere else — because those boundaries are not features they ship, not measured zeros. Competitor cells are COVERAGE marks (covered / not-offered / front-only / leads), never fabricated numbers; only fak's own column carries measured figures, every one tracing to fak/BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md by commit. Edit this file, then run `python tools/hero_matrix_gen.py`.

meta.title: fak — the agent kernel, vs the serving stacks (capability matrix)

title: fak spans the whole boundary; the serving stacks span one band of it
subtitle: Serving engines win raw throughput and front-prefix reuse — and stop there. fak's cache is addressable — it reaches into the middle of a kept run — so it does that reuse too, then proves it exact and makes the permission gate a syscall. An em-dash means a capability the stack does not ship, not a measured zero.

categories.name: SERVING REUSE
categories.note: the band the SOTA stacks own — fak matches it

rows.sub: keep the run from the first token

categories.name: SECURITY KERNEL — THE BOUNDARY THEY DON'T OWN

rows.sub: the cost of NOT owning it

categories.name: SINGLE STREAM — WHERE THEY LEAD
categories.note: shown, not hidden — fak does not target raw throughput

methodology.title: Evaluation methodology
notes: Only the fak column carries measured figures; every one traces to fak/BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md by the commit shown at the row's right. Machine: Apple M3 Pro 6P+6E 36 GB, app_version 0.30.0, llama.cpp b9707 on the same box.
An em-dash (—) marks a capability the stack does not ship — a structural gap, not a measured zero. “n/a” marks a row that does not apply to a hosted API cache.

Serving reuse: 86.7% KV-hit is reported against SGLang RadixAttention's published 50–99% band; 4.1× fleet serving is fak's own kernel held constant (so it isolates reuse, not kernel speed) — it is a [SOTA-style] result, not a green win. The naive re-prefill comparison (~60×) is deliberately omitted as a strawman.

Correctness: max|Δ|=0 is the L∞ between an evicted-span run and a clean run that never saw the span (TestBatchedDecodeMatchesSerial / mid-run eviction). Greedy parity is 7/7 exact tokens vs llama.cpp on identical Q8 weights.

Security kernel: 362 ns decide and 3.3 µs admit are in-process p50; 2,849× is in-process vs process-per-decide adjudication (n=100). The detector that flags injections is ≈100% evadable by design — the floor is the default-deny lock + the quarantine, not the classifier.

Single stream: fak explicitly does not target raw throughput — llama.cpp/Metal leads decode (17.3 vs 8.7 tok/s) and prefill. These rows invert the highlight on purpose: the SOTA stack owns the bold cell. One chat → use llama.cpp; an agent fleet → use fak.
